commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a followup-runner fallback-scoped latch and regression tests so embedded model-fallback retries suppress queued user-message persistence after the first candidate persists it.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source-level yes: current main reuses the same queued transcript prompt for each sequential fallback candidate, but no live OpenRouter reproduction was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer needs to add real behavior proof for the reported OpenRouter/followup failover path; this is not a safe ClawSweeper repair task.

target.title: fix(followup): suppress queued user persistence after first fallback candidate persists
target.body:
## Summary

- Latch queued user-message persistence inside the followup-runner fallback loop so model-fallback retries no longer re-append the same queued message to the session JSONL.
- Add regression tests covering both the persisted-on-first-attempt path (subsequent candidate must suppress) and the not-yet-persisted path (subsequent candidate must still record).

## Why

With `messages.queue.mode: "followup"`, `runWithModelFallback` invokes its `run` callback once per candidate model. Each invocation re-passed `queued.transcriptPrompt` into `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, and the embedded session manager appended the queued user message every time. The session ended up with consecutive `user`-role entries (2–4 copies), which providers reject with HTTP 400 (OpenRouter masks this as 500), triggering another failover cycle and a death spiral.

The fix: declare `let queuedUserMessagePersistedAcrossFallback = false;` outside the fallback callback. Each candidate's `runEmbeddedPiAgent` call passes `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence: (run.suppressNextUserMessagePersistence ?? false) || queuedUserMessagePersistedAcrossFallback` and an `onUserMessagePersisted` hook that flips the flag. After the first candidate persists the queued message, subsequent retries within the same fallback set leave the transcript untouched.

CLI runner branch is untouched: it does not accept `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence` and owns its own transcript management. Scope limited to the embedded path described in the issue (`openai-completions` via OpenRouter).

Refs: #83404

## Verification

Local:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts
```

Result: `Test Files 1 passed (1) | Tests 43 passed (43)`.

Filtered run of the new cases:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -t "queued user message idempotency across fallback"
```

Result: `2 passed | 41 skipped`.

Behavior addressed: queued followup user message duplicated in session JSONL on model-fallback retry.
Real environment tested: no — source + unit-level only. No live provider/channel reproduction was run.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: see vitest commands above.
Evidence after fix: new tests assert `runEmbeddedPiAgent` second-attempt call receives `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence: true` when the first attempt persisted the queued user message, and `false` when no persistence happened on the first attempt.
Observed result after fix: tests pass.
What was not tested: live OpenRouter failover repro; CLI runtime branch (out of scope — does not accept the suppression param).

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`
- [ ] live OpenRouter + followup + provoked upstream 500 (deferred — not reproducible locally)
target.author: yetval
